name: Healthie API
what_it_does: Digital health infrastructure API for telehealth and wellness platforms. Provides scheduling, charting, billing, patient engagement, and video sessions — a white-label EHR built for modern digital health companies.
use_cases: Building AI-assisted care coordination for telehealth platforms
Automating patient intake and scheduling workflows
Accessing clinical notes and charting data for clinical AI applications
Patient engagement automation via messaging and care plans
Billing and insurance verification for digital health practices
not_for: Traditional hospital EHR integration (use FHIR for Epic/Cerner)
Large health systems needing enterprise-grade EHR features
Medical imaging or diagnostic equipment integration
best_when: A digital health startup or telehealth company needs to build on top of a HIPAA-compliant health record platform without building EHR infrastructure from scratch.
avoid_when: You need to integrate with legacy hospital EHR systems or require enterprise-scale clinical workflow features.

auth.notes: API key for server-side integration; OAuth2 for user-context operations. Key passed via Authorization header. HIPAA Business Associate Agreement required before accessing PHI.

pricing.paid_tiers: Platform pricing starting around $99/month for solo practitioners; custom for enterprise
pricing.notes: Pricing based on number of providers and features needed. HIPAA compliance included. API access bundled with platform subscription. Enterprise pricing requires sales call.

performance.rate_limits: Generous for typical healthcare workloads; specific limits not published

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL error responses follow standard format. Error messages are sometimes generic. Documentation requires GraphQL knowledge to navigate effectively.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL mutations do not provide idempotency keys. Agents must implement their own duplicate prevention by checking for existing records before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA BAA must be signed before any PHI can be accessed — required before going to production
GraphQL schema is large and complex — agents need to understand healthcare data model
Patient identity linking across providers requires careful deduplication
Insurance verification calls out to external services and can be slow
Video session functionality requires Healthie's WebRTC infrastructure — not standalone
Rate limits undocumented — healthcare workflows typically fine, but unknown for high-volume automation
